zh.lifeSummary: 當Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach於1339年3月3日出生在可特格希 貝也藍書特時，他的父親Stephen II Duke of Bavaria25歲，母親Elisabeth de Sicile29歲。他於1360年與Anna Von Neuffen結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他於1374年被列為 reichslandvogt in oberschwaben。他去世於1393年12月4日在奧地利波麥布德維斯享年54歲，埋葬在美國堪薩斯州巴伯格藍。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach于1339年3月3日出生在可特格希 貝也藍書特时，他的父亲Stephen II Duke of Bavaria25岁，母亲Elisabeth de Sicile29岁。他于1360年与Anna Von Neuffen结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他于1374年被列为 reichslandvogt in oberschwaben。他去世于1393年12月4日在奥地利波麥布德維斯享年54岁，埋葬在美国堪萨斯州巴伯格藍。

nameMeanings.name: House
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name for someone employed ‘at the house’, from Middle English hous(e) (Old English hūs), probably a religious house such as a convent. In the Middle Ages the majority of the population lived in cottages or huts rather than houses.
English: variant of   Howes  .

English: perhaps a topographic name from an unrecorded Middle English huse, a southwest dialect form of Old English hyse ‘place overgrown with (water) plants’, a derivative of Old English hos ‘(water) plant’. In modern English, the name might have been absorbed into Huss or Hose .

variants.name: Huizenga
meanings: West Frisian and Dutch (mainly Groningen): habitational name from Huizinge in Groningen, a placename possibly composed with Middle Dutch huus, huse, Dutch huis ‘house’ (see House ).

variants.name: Houze
meanings: Altered form of   House   4, a surname of German origin (see   Haus  ).
French (Houzé): metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of boots and shoes or a nickname for someone noted for his footwear, from Old French ho(u)se ‘boot, shoe’.

variants.name: Howes
meanings: English:

pronounced to rhyme with hose, a variant of Howe 1 with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. Hose (Leicestershire), recorded as Howes in 1086, is named with the plural of Old English hōh ‘hill spur’.
pronounced to rhyme with cows, a variant of Howe 2 (see Hugh ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howse
meanings: English (Oxfordshire):
variant of   House  .
variants of   Howes  .

variants.name: Houseman
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English hous ‘house’ + man ‘man’, denoting someone employed at ‘the house’, probably a religious house or convent. Compare House .
English: altered form of Oulsnam (itself a variant of Wolstenholme ), with metathesis of final -nam to -man and development of inorganic initial H-.
Americanized form of German   Hausmann   or Dutch   Huisman  .

variants.name: Huse
meanings: North German: from Middle Low German hūs ‘house’ (see House 1), which may also refer to an important building such as the town hall, a castle, or a manor house, so that the name would indicate dwelling in close proximity to these, or perhaps it is an epithet for a servant who worked at a great house.
English (Berkshire): variant of   Hose   and possibly a shortened form of   Hussey  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Frederick,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Frederic
meanings: From German. See Friedrich and Frederick .

variants.name: Fredrick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredric
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

nameMeanings.name: Duke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: In modern use this normally represents a coinage parallel to   Earl   and   King  , but it has been used as a short form of   Marmaduke   from at least the early 1600s.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

en.lifeSummary: When Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach was born on 3 March 1339, in Landshut, Bavaria, his father, Stephen II Duke of Bavaria, was 25 and his mother, Elisabeth de Sicile, was 29. He married Anna Von Neuffen in 1360. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. In 1374, at the age of 35, his occupation is listed as  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben. He died on 4 December 1393, in Budweis, Bohemia, Austria, at the age of 54, and was buried in Gerlane, Barber, Kansas, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach est né le 3 mars 1339, à Landshut, Königreich Bayern, son père, Stephen II Duke of Bavaria, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth de Sicile, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Anna Von Neuffen en 1360. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. En 1374, à l’âge de 35 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben. Il est décédé le 4 décembre 1393, à Budweis, Bohême, Autriche, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Gerlane, Barber, Kansas, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach am 3. März 1339 in Landshut, Königreich Bayern geboren wurde, war sein Vater Stephen II Duke of Bavaria 25 und seine Mutter Elisabeth de Sicile 29. Er hat 1360 Anna Von Neuffen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. 1374, als er 35 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben aufgeführt. Er starb am 4. Dezember 1393 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Budweis, Böhmen, Österreich und wurde in Gerlane, Barber, Kansas, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach è nato il il 3 marzo 1339 nel Landshut, Königreich Bayern, il suo padre, Stephen II Duke of Bavaria, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Elisabeth de Sicile, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Anna Von Neuffen nel 1360. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Nel 1374, all'età di 35 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben. Morì il 4 dicembre 1393, nel Budweis, Böhmen, Austria, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Gerlane, Barber, Kansas, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbachが1339年3月3日にクoニグレイチ バイエルンランドシュトで生まれた時，父Stephen II Duke of Bavariaは25歳，母Elisabeth de Sicileは29歳であった。1360年にAnna Von Neuffenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。1374年，35歳の時，職業は reichslandvogt in oberschwabenと記録されている。彼は1393年12月4日にオーストリアブoメンブドウエイスで54歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国カンザス州バーバージャーランにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach(이)가 1339년 3월 3일 ko니그레이치 베이른 랜드슈트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Stephen II Duke of Bavaria(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth de Sicile(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1360년 Anna Von Neuffen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1374년, 35세의 나이로 그의 직업은  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben이었다. 그는 1393년 12월 4일 오스트리아 브oh멘 버드위스에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 캔자스 주 바버 그를랜에 묻혔다.

pt.Kloster Seligenthal, Landshut, Landshute: Gerlane, Barber, Cansas, Estados Unidos da América
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach nasceu em 3 de março de 1339, em Landshut, Königreich Bayern, seu pai, Stephen II Duke of Bavaria, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth de Sicile, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Anna Von Neuffen em 1360. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Em 1374, com 35 anos, sua profissão aparece como  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben. Ele faleceu em 4 de dezembro de 1393, em Budweis, Böhmen, Áustria, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Gerlane, Barber, Cansas, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach родился 3 марта 1339 г. в местности ландшут, кoниграич беиирн, его отцу по имени Stephen II Duke of Bavaria было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth de Sicile, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Von Neuffen в 1360 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. В 1374 году, в возрасте 35 лет,  он работал по профессии  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben. Он умер 4 декабря 1393 г. в местности будвис, бoхмен, Австрия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности джирлан, барбир, Канзас, Соединенные штаты.

es.03 March 1339–04 December 1393: 3 de marzo de 1339–4 de diciembre de 1393
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Frederick, Duke of Bavaria House of Wittelsbach nació el 3 de marzo de 1339, en Landshut, Königreich Bayern, su papá, Stephen II Duke of Bavaria, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elisabeth de Sicile, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Anna Von Neuffen en 1360. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. En 1374, a la edad de 35 años, su ocupación registrada era  reichslandvogt in oberschwaben. Murió el 4 de diciembre de 1393, en Budweis, Böhmen, Austria, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Gerlane, Barber, Kansas, Estados Unidos.
